task_name: add Mistral Instruct v3 chat template

details.reasoning: The implementation has correct structural elements: function definition, auto-detection logic with proper [SYSTEM_PROMPT] check, LOG_DBG call, and COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY format. However, it fails on core functional requirements. The handler doesn't implement the actual template behavior - it just calls common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl without any logic to: wrap user turns with [INST]/[/INST] markers, prepend system messages to the first user turn, or append </s> after assistant turns. The function appears to be an empty wrapper relying entirely on template strings rather than implementing the format logic itself, which contradicts how similar handlers typically work. This is approximately 50% structurally correct but fundamentally incomplete in implementation.
